{{Short description|2024 horror film by Curry Barker}}
{{Infobox film
| name           = Milk & Serial
| image          = Milk & Serial.jpg
| alt            = 
| caption        = Official [[YouTube]] thumbnail
| director       = [[Curry Barker]]
| writer         = Curry Barker
| producer       = {{Plainlist|
* Cooper Tomlinson
* Curry Barker
}}
| starring       = {{Plainlist|
* Curry Barker
* Cooper Tomlinson
* Adlih Alvarado
}}
| editing        = Curry Barker
| cinematography = {{Plainlist|
* Curry Barker
* Cooper Tomlinson
}}
| music          = Curry Barker
| studio         = That's a Bad Idea
| distributor    = That's a Bad Idea<br>{{small|(via [[YouTube]])}}
| released       = {{Film date|2024|08|08|}}
| runtime        = 62 minutes
| country        = United States
| language       = English
| budget         = $800
}}'''''Milk & Serial''''' is a 2024 American [[horror film]] written and directed by [[Curry Barker]], in his directorial debut. The [[Found footage (film technique)|found footage]] film stars Barker and Cooper Tomlinson, the [[Double act|comedy duo]] best known for their YouTube [[sketch comedy]] channel "that's a bad idea<!--Stylized in lower case-->". With a budget of $800, the duo made it in four months. After failing to work with a film distributor, Barker decided to release the full movie on to [[YouTube]] for free. It was released on August 8, 2024. ''Milk & Serial'' received positive reviews from critics.

== Plot ==
Best friends and roommates Marshall "Milk" and Steve "Seven" run a prank channel together on YouTube. Before Milk's birthday party at their apartment, Seven films himself buying a gun and blanks to prank him. 

The party is attended by several friends, including Link, Naomi, and Naomi's girlfriend Lara. During a game of "[[Never have I ever|Never Have I Ever]]", Milk admits to killing a cat, making everyone else uncomfortable. Seven's improv friend, hired by him for the prank, arrives and shoots Naomi with a blank, impressing Milk. Later, their elderly neighbor Greg knocks to complain about the noise. Afterward, while returning from a trip to [[7-Eleven]], the friends see Greg standing motionless on a corner, which Milk dismisses as another prank. 

The next day, Seven and Milk encounter Greg sitting on their couch downstairs. Milk goes to apologize, only to find that Greg has gone upstairs, where he is having a breakdown. Milk calms Greg by promising to have dinner with him, but before they leave, a heartbroken Naomi arrives, claiming Lara broke up with her via text.

Milk and Seven dine with Greg at his apartment, where Greg seemingly drugs them before tying them up and driving them to a remote desert. After Seven shoots Greg in self-defense, Milk reveals that he orchestrated the entire situation as a prank; Greg is a hired actor, and the gun Seven used is the same one from the birthday prank. Seven is thrilled at the prospect of increased viewership from the prank, but his excitement dissipates upon discovering that Greg is actually dead; Seven blames the man who sold him the gun.

Past footage reveals that Milk abducted Lara after the party and tied her up in a secret hideout, then used her phone to break up with Naomi. Milk confesses to the camera that he is jealous of Seven's popularity on their channel, and has come up with an elaborate plan to frame Seven for murder as revenge, which involved swapping the blanks with real bullets.

In the present, Milk gets Seven to bury Greg's body. The next day, Milk gaslights a traumatized Seven and provokes him into beating him. Milk then goes to bludgeon Lara to death. After Greg's son Frank confronts them about Greg's disappearance, they use Greg's phone to make it look like Greg went to Las Vegas. When this backfires, Milk convinces Seven to go to Frank's house to distract him so Milk can stab him to death. Having had enough of Milk's destructive actions and apparent lack of remorse at having caused the deaths of two people, Seven angrily declares that he is quitting the channel.

Upon learning that Seven left the channel, Link and Naomi decide to investigate why Seven, and discover a box under Milk's bed filled with vials of blood, each one labeled with a different person's name. The box also contains a key, which leads them to Milk's lair and Lara's dead body.

As Milk tries to clean up the blood, a guilt-ridden Seven begs Milk to kill him or turn him in. Milk gleefully speaks to the camera, telling the viewer that he wants to be remembered as the most creative serial killer in history, and that he believes he has accomplished this goal by not directly killing Seven, but instead creating a series of circumstances that he hopes will lead to Seven killing himself. Seven walks in and shoots Milk, who subsequently drops the camera. Another gunshot rings out, indicating Seven shot then shot himself, the credits rolling before the still-alive Milk picks the camera back up.{{efn|Milk having earlier mentioned that he had left a blank at the front of the gun, which he was shot with before Seven shot himself.}}

== Cast ==
* [[Curry Barker]] as Milk
* Cooper Tomlinson as Seven
* Adlih Alvarado as Naomi 
* Sterling L. Pope as Link
* Jonnathon Cripple as Greg
* John Simmonds as Frank 
* Gloria Karel as Lara
* Andy Dubitsky as Arms Dealer
* Tristan Welsh as Jason
* Paul Santoli as Paul
* Rob Harrow as Cop

Credits adapted from ''[[Bloody Disgusting]]''.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |last=Drew |first=Ari |title='Milk & Serial' - New Found Footage Movie Is One of This Year's Hidden Horror Gems - Bloody Disgusting |url=https://bloody-disgusting.com/editorials/3826876/milk-serial-new-found-footage-movie-is-one-of-this-years-best-kept-secrets/ |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20260101202929/https://bloody-disgusting.com/editorials/3826876/milk-serial-new-found-footage-movie-is-one-of-this-years-best-kept-secrets/ |archive-date=2026-01-01 |access-date=2026-03-30 |website=bloody-disgusting.com |language=en-US}}</ref>

== Production and release ==
[[File:Curry Barker 2026 Century City.jpg|thumb|Curry Barker in 2026]]
''Milk & Serial'' was made by the [[Double act|comedy duo]] [[Curry Barker]] and Cooper Tomlinson.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=Earl |first=William |date=2024-08-26 |title=How ‘Milk & Serial,’ a Clever Slasher Made for $800 and Released for Free on YouTube, Became a Must-Watch for Horror Fans |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/news/milk-serial-curry-barker-slasher-youtube-1236116310/ |access-date=2026-03-30 |website=Variety |language=en-US}}</ref> The duo had previously gathered a fan base on [[YouTube]] with their [[sketch comedy]] channel "that's a bad Idea<!--Stylized in lower case-->".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Couch |first=Aaron |date=2025-09-10 |title=A Year Ago, He Was Making $800 YouTube Movies. Now He's Sold a Horror Pic For Millions |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-features/obsession-deal-curry-barker-movie-1236367298/ |access-date=2026-03-30 |website=The Hollywood Reporter |language=en-US}}</ref> The channel also occasionally did other projects, including the 2023 viral horror short film "The Chair".<ref name=":1" /> ''Milk & Serial'' was the duo's first feature in the genre.<ref name=":0" /> The budget for ''Milk & Serial'' was [[Dollar sign|$]]800, and was primarily spent on hiring an actor and buying a [[Sony camcorders|Sony camcorder]] camera.<ref name=":2">{{Cite news |last=Heritage |first=Stuart |date=2024-08-28 |title=Milk & Serial: the vicious, viral $800-budget horror that's free to watch |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/article/2024/aug/28/milk-serial-horror-youtube-review |access-date=2026-03-30 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref> ''Milk & Serial'' was shot over four months, with help from their friends playing other roles and working weekends depending on everyone's availability.<ref name=":1" />

After a year of trying to get [[film distributor]] for ''Milk & Serial'', Barker decided to release the full movie on to video-sharing platform [[YouTube]] for free. In an interview with ''[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]'', Barker said "So even though we worked really hard for a year trying to get distribution for this thing, we said screw it and threw it on YouTube."<ref name=":1" /> ''Milk & Serial'' was released on August 8, 2024.<ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" />

== Reception ==
''Milk & Serial'' received positive reviews from critics. Stuart Heritage of ''[[The Guardian]]'' praised the story, and said "As a film it's great. There are some incredibly neatly observed moments, such as when the main character describes [[gaslighting]] as 'when you prank somebody but make them think it's their fault'." Heritage compared it to other found footage film like ''[[The Blair Witch Project]]'', ''[[Paranormal Activity]],'' and [[Rob Savage]]'s ''[[Host (film)|Host]]''.<ref name=":2" /> Timothy Rawles of ''iHorror'' said "Move over ''[[Longlegs]]'' and [[Cuckoo (2024 film)|''Cuckoo'']], one of the most disturbing movies of the year is actually on YouTube. For free."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Rawles |first=Timothy |date=2024-08-27 |title=YouTube Found-Footage Horror Short Might Be One of the Best Movies of the Year |url=https://ihorror.com/youtube-found-footage-horror-short-might-be-one-of-the-best-movies-of-the-year/ |access-date=2026-03-30 |website=iHorror |language=en-US}}</ref>

Mary McAndrews of [[Dread Central]] named ''Milk & Serial'' as among the "best found footage films of 2024" and said "It's a simple story supported by some harrowing performances and clever editing."<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAndrews |first=Mary Beth |date=2024-12-28 |title=The 10 Best Found Footage Horror Films of 2024 |url=https://www.dreadcentral.com/editorials/519087/the-10-best-found-footage-horror-films-of-2024/ |access-date=2026-03-30 |website=Dread Central |language=en-US}}</ref> William Earl of ''[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]'' said "Curry Barker has delivered the year's most unlikely hit."<ref name=":1" /> Yonni Uribe of ''Slug Mag'' said "This film is not one to miss if you're a horror nerd with an affinity for found footage." Uribe compared it to other horror internet projects made by [[Chris Stuckmann]] and [[Kane Parsons|Kane Pixels]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Barnhart |first=Alton |date=2024-09-11 |title=Film Review: Milk & Serial - SLUG Magazine |url=https://www.slugmag.com/arts/film/film-reviews/film-review-milk-serial/ |access-date=2026-03-30 |website=slugmag.com |language=en-US}}</ref> Ari Drew of [[Bloody Disgusting]] said it is "one of the year's best-kept secrets".<ref name=":2" />

== Notes ==
{{notelist}}

== References==
{{Reflist}}

== External links ==
* {{YouTube|pbzGQ1lszv4|''Milk & Serial'' (Found Footage Horror Film)}}
* {{IMDb title|tt22075376}}

{{Curry Barker}}

[[Category:2024 YouTube videos]]
[[Category:2024 films]]
[[Category:Films released on YouTube]]
[[Category:Found_footage_horror_films]]
[[Category:2024 horror films]]
[[Category:Films about pranks]]
[[Category:Films directed by Curry Barker]]
